---
title: "Alpha Diversity"
author: "Nitin Bayal"
format: html
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Introduction

This document reports on a microbiota project results of alpha diversity across different diets and visits.

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
 #STEP1: alpha diversity
# # Loop through indices and comparisons to create and save plots
# final_results <- run_diversity_tests(tse, comparisons, variable, indices, outdir)
# # View the final merged results
# print(final_results)
# for (index in indices) {
#   lapply(comparisons, function(comp) create_diversity_plot(tse, comp, index,outdir))
# }

# Define variables
variable <- "group"  
```

**Group variable: `r variable`**

```{r data-load, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# tse <- readRDS("../data/tse.Rds")
source("data.R")
```

## Data Summary

```{r data-summary, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
tse
```

## Observed Species

```{r observed-species, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height= 5, fig.width= 8, echo=FALSE}

# Estimate (observed species) richness
tse <- addAlpha(x = tse, assay.type = "counts", index = c("observed"))

# Convert colData to a data frame for ggplot2
df <- as.data.frame(colData(tse))

# Visualization - boxplot with jittered points
p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = group, y = observed)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Add boxplot
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +  # Add jittered points
  labs(
    x = "Groups",
    y = expression(Richness[Observed])
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),  # Rotate x-axis labels
    text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

p

```

## Group-wise comparisons using Shannon index

```{r group-wise-comparisons-shannon, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.height= 5, fig.width= 8, echo=FALSE}

# Define index and grouping variable
index <- "shannon"

# Ensure the grouping variable is a factor
colData(tse)[[variable]] <- factor(colData(tse)[[variable]])

# Calculate p-values
pvals <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
  x = colData(tse)[[index]],
  g = colData(tse)[[variable]],
  p.adjust.method = "fdr"
)

# Convert p-values to a data frame for ggpubr
pvals_df <- as.data.frame(pvals$p.value)
pvals_df$group1 <- rownames(pvals_df)
pvals_long <- pivot_longer(pvals_df, -group1, names_to = "group2", values_to = "p") %>%
  na.omit()

# Add y-axis position
pvals_long <- pvals_long %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = sapply(1:nrow(.), function(i) {
      max(c(
        colData(tse)[[index]][colData(tse)[[variable]] == .$group1[i]],
        colData(tse)[[index]][colData(tse)[[variable]] == .$group2[i]]
      ))
    })
  )
pvals_long$y.position <- max(pvals_long$y.position) + seq_len(nrow(pvals_long)) * 0.4
pvals_long$p <- round(pvals_long$p, 3)

# Filter for significant p-values (p < 0.05)
pvals_long <- pvals_long %>% filter(p < 0.05)

# Visualization using ggplot2 - boxplot with jitter plot (no color)
library(ggplot2)
df <- as.data.frame(colData(tse))  # Convert colData to a data frame for ggplot2

p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[variable]], y = .data[[index]])) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Add boxplot
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +  # Add jittered points
  stat_pvalue_manual(pvals_long, label = "p") +  # Add significant p-value annotations
  labs(
    x = "Groups",
    y = "Shannon Index"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # Rotate X-axis labels
    text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

p
```

### Comparison of the microbial community composition between groups

```{r echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyverse)


index <- "shannon"

# Calculate p values
pvals <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
    tse[[index]], tse[[variable]], p.adjust.method = "fdr")
# Put them to data.frame format
pvals <- pvals[["p.value"]] |>
    as.data.frame()
varname <- "group1"
pvals[[varname]] <- rownames(pvals)
# To long format
pvals <- reshape(
    pvals,
    direction = "long",
    varying = colnames(pvals)[ !colnames(pvals) %in% varname ],
    times = colnames(pvals)[ !colnames(pvals) %in% varname ],
    v.names = "p",
    timevar = "group2",
    idvar = "group1"
    ) |>
    na.omit()
# Add y-axis position
pvals[["y.position"]] <- apply(pvals, 1, function(x){
    temp1 <- tse[[index]][ tse[[variable]] == x[["group1"]] ]
    temp2 <- tse[[index]][ tse[[variable]] == x[["group2"]] ]
    temp <- max( c(temp1, temp2) )
    return(temp)
})
pvals[["y.position"]] <- max(pvals[["y.position"]]) +
    order(pvals[["y.position"]]) * 0.2
# Round values
pvals[["p"]] <- round(pvals[["p"]], 3)

# Create a boxplot
p <- plotColData(
    tse, x = variable, y = index,
    show_boxplot = TRUE, show_violin = FALSE) +
    theme(text = element_text(size = 10)) +
    stat_pvalue_manual(pvals)
p
```
